title: Theorem database extract
description: Canonical JSON artifact produced by the Lean extractor. Normalized node/edge form: declarations and informal-results are nodes; states_with / formal_uses / informal_uses are edges. SQLite and Kuzu are both loaded from this single artifact.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of THIS schema. Bump on any breaking change to record shapes.

git_commit.description: Commit of the corpus the extract was built from.

declarations.description: Primary nodes: one per Lean theorem/lemma/def. Custom defs targeted by states_with also appear here.

informal_results.description: Second node class: registry stubs for real-world results not yet formalized.

groups.description: Deduplicated rollup of problem-level facts (ref / attribution / extern) by group key, plus drift detection. Derived; not authored.

edges.description: Single source of truth for dependency edges. A declaration's deps are recovered by filtering on `from`. Not duplicated inline on declarations.

stats.description: Optional summary counts for sanity-checking an extract.

attribution.description: A who/when credit. `who` is a plain string (person-name fragmentation accepted; see plan.md). `year` optional.

decl_kind.description: Lean declaration keyword. `axiom` records a literature-proved result asserted on the authority of its `@[ref]` citation (not yet formalized).

category.description: null when the declaration carries no `category` attribute (e.g. a bare def).

ams.description: AMS subject codes (the integers from @[AMS n]).

statement_tex.description: The /-- docstring -/ verbatim: the LaTeX statement and gloss. `refs` pin its provenance.

type.description: Pretty-printed Lean type of the declaration.

refs.description: Opaque reference strings from @[ref "..."]. Decoration, NOT graph nodes; no validation, may fragment.

group.description: Variant-cluster key from @[group "..."]. null for a standalone declaration.

formalization.description: Auto-derived; never authored. `uses_sorry` and `axioms` come from `#print axioms`; `status` additionally consults each non-standard axiom's `@[ref]` tags to decide whether it is literature-backed.

uses_sorry.description: True iff the proof term depends on `sorryAx` (the machine-checkable genuine-gap flag).

status.description: 'proved' iff every axiom is one of the standard foundational axioms (propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound). 'cited' iff there is ≥1 non-standard axiom and every non-standard axiom carries a literature `@[ref]` (a result asserted on the authority of its citation, e.g. a published theorem recorded as an `axiom`). 'stated_only' otherwise: depends on `sorryAx`, or on a custom axiom with no `@[ref]` — a genuine gap.

axioms.description: Axioms the proof depends on, e.g. sorryAx, propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound, or a literature-backed corpus axiom. Drives `status`.

informalResult.description: Registry node: a real-world result with no Lean Name (yet). Target of informal_uses edges.

key.description: Canonical registry key, e.g. "riemann-hypothesis". Primary key. informal_uses references this.

formalized_as.description: Once promoted/formalized, the Lean declaration that now states this. Links the string node to a real declaration.

group.description: Derived rollup of one variant cluster. Problem-level facts are authored on every member declaration; the extractor deduplicates them here and reports disagreement in `conflicts`.

conflicts.description: Group-consistency lint output: problem-level fields whose values disagree across members.

values.description: Map of member name -> that member's value for the conflicting field.

kind.description: states_with: auto, from type's custom constants. formal_uses: manual, proof-level, Name target. informal_uses: manual, registry-key target.

from.description: The declaration the edge originates from.

to.description: A Lean Name (states_with, formal_uses) or an informal-result registry key (informal_uses).

to_type.description: Which node class `to` references. declaration for states_with/formal_uses; informal_result for informal_uses.

resolved.description: True iff `to` resolves to an existing node. Always true for states_with/formal_uses (build enforces). May be false for informal_uses (unknown registry key -> build warning).
